title: cyathea kessleriana
intro: Cyathea kessleriana, also known as the Kessler's tree fern, is a species of tree fern that belongs to the Cyathea genus of ferns. Traditionally used in folk medicine, this plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Cyathea kessleriana are numerous, and it is often used to treat various ailments, such as bronchitis, coughs, and skin conditions, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Kessler's tree fern are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are also applied topically to the skin as a poultice to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
